item.title: Who could replace Lindsey Graham in the Senate after his sudden death?
item.description: South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham's sudden death late Saturday has set off a scramble for who will succeed him in the Senate.

item.title: Military action against Iran formally restarted last week, Trump told lawmakers
item.description: President Trump formally notified Congress that "military action" against Iran restarted last week in a letter obtained by CBS News, as a monthslong ceasefire comes to an end.

item.title: Expert says neither side controls Strait of Hormuz as fighting intensifies between U.S. and Iran
item.description: Fighting between the U.S. and Iran over control of the Strait of Hormuz intensified Monday as both sides claimed possession, with President Trump reinstating his blockade on Iranian ships. Mona Yacoubian, director and senior adviser of the Middle East Program at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, joins CBS News to share her analysis.

item.title: Darline Graham Nordone, Lindsey Graham's sister, appointed to fill Senate seat
item.description: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster announced his pick to fill the vacancy left by Sen. Lindsey Graham's death.

item.title: ICE agent shoots and kills motorist in Maine, senator says
item.description: Maine Sen. Angus King said he told Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin that he wanted a transparent investigation into the shooting.

item.title: Lawmakers remember Lindsey Graham on Senate floor
item.description: The Senate floor opened Monday for the first time since the death of Sen. Lindsey Graham over the weekend, with lawmakers paying tribute to their colleague. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns provided context.

item.title: Trump tells Congress Iran fighting has resumed, vows to "keep" Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump says the U.S. will "probably run" the Strait of Hormuz, as dueling attacks with Iran continue over control of the strategic waterway.

item.title: Judge rebukes Trump and DOJ over IRS lawsuit in scathing decision
item.description: U.S. District Judge Kathleen Williams said President Trump's lawsuit against the IRS had been filed for an "improper purpose."

item.title: Stench from Los Angeles warehouse fire lingers in air as cleanup continues
item.description: Los Angeles residents continue to deal with the stench of burned waste after a massive warehouse fire as cleanup gets underway. CBS News Los Angeles reporter Rick Montanez reports.

item.title: Bison charges man, flips him in the air at Yellowstone National Park
item.description: Warning: graphic footage. A bison charged a man at Yellowstone National Park last Friday, flipping him in the air. The incident was captured on video. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: South Carolina lawmakers pay tribute to Lindsey Graham after his shocking death
item.description: South Carolina lawmakers, including Sen. Tim Scott, Reps. Nancy Mace and William Timmons, and Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette, paid tribute to Sen. Lindsey Graham, who died suddenly at 71 on Saturday.

item.title: 12 states sue to block Paramount-Warner Bros. Discovery merger
item.description: The suit poses a new challenge to the $110 billion deal that would unite two of the nation's largest media companies.

item.title: 1 person killed in Maine shooting involving ICE, state House speaker says
item.description: A person was shot and killed in Maine involving ICE, the state's House speaker said Monday. The shooting comes after an ICE agent fatally shot a Mexican man in Houston, Texas, last week. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul reports.

item.title: Notable Deaths in 2026
item.description: A look back at the esteemed personalities who've left us this year, who'd touched us with their innovation, creativity and humanity.

item.title: Sources: FBI fires 2 analysts who raised concerns about Georgia 2020 election probe
item.description: The two analysts expressed concerns that the 2020 election investigation in Fulton County, Georgia, was thin on evidence, sources said.

item.title: How much interest will a $50,000 1-year CD earn if opened this July?
item.description: Locking in the right CD rate now could earn your savings thousands of dollars in interest over the next year.

item.title: Innocent bystander shot by police is one of dozens of cases nationwide
item.description: CBS News reviewed police records, body camera footage, court documents and local news reports to find more than 50 cases of innocent bystanders shot by police.

item.title: AI companies are creating "generative ghosts" of deceased loved ones
item.description: Startups are using emails, photos and voice recordings to create AI simulations that family and friends can interact with after a loved one's death.

item.title: Police shootings of innocent bystanders aren't tracked by federal agencies, investigation finds
item.description: A CBS News investigation found that no federal agency is tracking police shootings of innocent bystanders, making it difficult to hold officers accountable. CBS News' Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

item.title: Family says they're forced to sell home to help power data centers
item.description: Georgia Power says building a new transmission line will require acquiring more than 300 parcels of land, including residential properties.

item.title: Georgia homeowners are being forced from their homes to help power AI data centers
item.description: Some homeowners in Georgia say they have been given an ultimatum: sell your home or the state will take it. Utility giant Georgia Power is planning to build a new transmission line so it can handle more data centers. Skyler Henry reports.

item.title: Trump backs Lindsey Graham's sister to finish Senate term
item.description: President Trump recommended that South Carolina's governor appoint the late Sen. Lindsey Graham's sister Darline to serve out the rest of his term in the Senate.

item.title: Egg producers reach federal and state settlement after price-fixing probe
item.description: Three U.S. egg producers will be required to provide 53 million eggs to food banks and to pay a $3.3 million financial penalty.

item.title: Congress returns with long to-do list as Graham's death hangs over Capitol
item.description: Congress is returning to Washington with limited time to address a number of priorities ahead of a lengthy August recess and the sprint to the midterm elections.

item.title: Bison tosses man 8 feet in the air at Yellowstone, video shows
item.description: A 65-year-old man was thrown 8 feet into the air by bison who charged at him in Yellowstone National Park, video shows.

item.title: Aircraft goes down in reservoir while fighting fire; 1 body recovered
item.description: A body was recovered from a firefighting aircraft that went down in Silver Jack Reservoir in western Colorado.

item.title: Common knee surgery may do more harm than good, 10-year study shows
item.description: A Finnish study followed patients for 10 years after they had a popular knee surgery. For many, the pain continued or even worsened.

item.title: 7/12: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Sen. Tim Scott, Israeli Ambassador Michael Leiter, Rep. Mike Turner and Rahm Emanuel pay tribute to Sen. Lindsey Graham after his sudden death.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for July 12, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include White House border czar Tom Homan and retired Gen. Frank McKenzie, president of The Citadel and former commander of U.S. Central Command.

item.title: 12-year-old injured by bison at Yellowstone National Park
item.description: A 12-year-old was injured by a bison while visiting Yellowstone National Park last week, the National Park Service said.
